response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack and distributed systems engineer with strong software craftsmanship practices, but he critically lacks the core minimum qualification for this role: 8 years of C++ experience. His C++ exposure is limited to a 6-month stint writing components at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a student thesis project — nowhere near the required depth. His career is predominantly TypeScript/Node.js/web product engineering; while he has touched large-scale microservices and some cloud tooling (AWS CDK, Kubernetes), he has not owned or led a cloud compute platform. There is also no evidence of formal technical leadership or mentoring of cloud/DevOps engineers at scale.
strengths: Demonstrated experience with high-throughput distributed systems at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second, low-latency async I/O, real-time metadata) shows relevant infrastructure instincts
Consistent application of engineering best practices — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, pairing — across multiple senior and staff roles
Breadth of technical exposure across embedded systems, mobile, web, DevOps, and AI-agent infrastructure reflects strong systems-level adaptability
concerns: C++ requirement is a hard minimum at 8 years; the candidate's actual C++ experience totals under 1 year spread across a student project and one 6-month role in 2014–2015
No evidence of owning or architecting a cloud compute platform; AWS CDK and Kubernetes appear only as supporting tooling within product-focused roles, not as primary infrastructure deliverables
No documented track record of formal technical leadership or mentoring of cloud/DevOps engineers, which is both a preferred qualification and a core responsibility of this role
